title: epipactis tremolsii subsp. lusitanica
intro: Epipactis tremolsii subsp. lusitanica, also known as Lusitanian helleborine, is a rare species of orchid native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant is known to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and soothing digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Epipactis tremolsii subsp. lusitanica are typically dried and made into infusions, tinctures, or salves to utilize its medicinal properties.
